amendments.normalized_body: BUG: In `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:4634-4636`, the pre-backoff snapshot code sets `snapshot.active_run = None` before sleeping. After the sleep, the loop continues back to line 4473, which calls `carry_forward_active_run(snapshot, ...)`. This function calls `current_active_run(snapshot)` (line 590), which requires `snapshot.active_run.is_some()` and returns `Err(AppError::CorruptRecord)` when it's `None`. Every retry with non-zero backoff will immediately fail with a CorruptRecord error, making the backoff feature worse than useless — it converts a retryable failure into a fatal one. Fix: after the sleep (and before `continue`), restore `snapshot.active_run` from `snapshot.interrupted_run` (which was saved by `preserve_interrupted_run` at line 4634) and reset `snapshot.status = RunStatus::Running`.

Missing test coverage for the backoff snapshot round-trip. The new backoff snapshot code at `engine.rs:4622-4664` is a critical path (it's the only code path that sets `active_run = None` mid-retry-loop) but has no integration or unit test. Add a test that exercises a stage failure with backoff enabled, verifying that: (1) the snapshot written during backoff has `status = Failed` and `active_run = None`, (2) after the backoff sleep, the retry loop successfully restores state and re-enters the stage, and (3) if the process is killed during backoff, `run resume` can recover from the persisted snapshot.

Update bead `ralph-burning-wt3` in `.beads/issues.jsonl:100` to explicitly define how final-review quorum changes when a reviewer backend becomes unavailable at runtime. The current code only skips failing reviewers when `member.required == false` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:225-235`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:427-437`) and still fails if executed reviewers/voters drop below `min_reviewers` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:281-286`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:473-478`). With the defaults, `DEFAULT_MIN_REVIEWERS` is 2 and both final-review backends are required (`src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs:25`, `src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs:1473-1477`), so the acceptance criterion to proceed with only one reviewer is impossible unless the bead also changes required-member handling and the effective minimum/consensus rules.

Expand the implementation scope described in `.beads/issues.jsonl:100` so “apply to all multi-backend stages” covers the actual runtime-failure paths outside final review. `completion` currently propagates the first invocation error from any completer and only checks `min_completers` after successful executions (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs:109-182`), and `prompt_review` does the same for validator failures (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:165-237`). If the new failure class is only handled in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs`, the same persistent backend outage will still abort other multi-backend panels.

Tighten the backend-unavailable detection requirements in `.beads/issues.jsonl:100` so classification only inspects backend-generated error channels on failed invocations, not arbitrary stdout text. `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1478-1487` already treats stdout as an error only when the backend sets `is_error == true`; otherwise stdout can contain ordinary model output. Matching broad substrings like `billing` or `credits` across raw stdout/stderr would create false positives for normal task content.

final_accepted_amendments.normalized_body: BUG: In `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:4634-4636`, the pre-backoff snapshot code sets `snapshot.active_run = None` before sleeping. After the sleep, the loop continues back to line 4473, which calls `carry_forward_active_run(snapshot, ...)`. payload.summary: Final review accepted 5 amendment(s); restart required.
